enumDefs.description: TermRelationshipStatus defines how reliable the relationship is between two glossary terms

elementDefs.description: DRAFT means the relationship is under development.
ACTIVE means the relationship is validated and in use.
DEPRECATED means the the relationship is being phased out.
OBSOLETE means that the relationship should not be used anymore.
OTHER means that there is another status.

enumDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryTermRelationshipStatus

enumDefs.description: The deployment status of a ML model build
enumDefs.name: ml_model_deployment_status

enumDefs.description: TermAssignmentStatus defines how much the semantic assignment should be trusted.

elementDefs.description: DISCOVERED means that the semantic assignment was added by a discovery engine.
PROPOSED means that the semantic assignment was proposed by person - they may be a subject matter expert, or consumer of the Referenceable asset
IMPORTED means that the semantic assignment has been imported from outside of the open metadata cluster
VALIDATED means that the semantic assignment has been reviewed and is highly trusted.
DEPRECATED means that the semantic assignment is being phased out. There may be another semantic assignment to the Referenceable that will ultimately replace this one.
OBSOLETE means that the semantic assignment is no longer in use,
OTHER means that the semantic assignment value does not match any of the other Term Assignment Status values

enumDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryTermAssignmentStatus

enumDefs.description: The storage type for an Ozone bucket
enumDefs.name: ozone_storage_type

enumDefs.description: The replication type for an Ozone key
enumDefs.name: ozone_replication_type

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the data entity and its business data steward
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_data_entity_steward_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the system to other systems
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_system_parent_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: This relationship demonstrates the connection between the referenced objects on Atlas to those in Archimate
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_referenceable_archimate_reference_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the data domain and the domain lead
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_domainLead_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the data entity to other data entities
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_parent_entity_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the data attribute and its business data owner
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_data_attribute_business_owner_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the data entity and its business data owner
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_data_entity_business_owner_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: A list of the most recent added/updated relationships with the source
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_referenceable_source_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: CategoryHierarchyLink is a relationship used to organize categories into a hierarchy to, for example, create a structure for a taxonomy. A category may have none or one super-categories. This super-category may be in a different glossary.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryCategoryHierarchyLink

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the data domain and the data entity
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_data_entity_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: TermAnchor links each term to exactly one Glossary object. This means that this is its home glossary. If the Glossary object is deleted, then so are all of the terms linked to it.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryTermAnchor

relationshipDefs.description: CategoryAnchor links each category to exactly one Glossary object. This means that this is its home glossary. If the Glossary object is deleted then so are all of the categories linked to it.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryCategoryAnchor

attributeDefs.description: The description field can be used to add details about the relationship.
attributeDefs.name: description

attributeDefs.description: The steward is the person responsible for assessing the relationship and deciding if it should be approved or not.
attributeDefs.name: steward

relationshipDefs.description: Antonym is a relationship between glossary terms that have the opposite (or near opposite) meaning, in the same language. Notice that both ends of this relationship have the same name and refer to the same type; this results in one Antonym attribute being added to GlossaryTerm.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryAntonym

attributeDefs.description: The description field can be used to add details about the relationship.
attributeDefs.name: description

attributeDefs.description: The steward is the person responsible for assessing the relationship and deciding if it should be approved or not.
attributeDefs.name: steward

relationshipDefs.description: PreferredTerm is a relationship that indicates that the preferredTerm should be used in place of the preferredToTerm. This relationship can be used to encourage adoption of newer vocabularies. This is a weaker version of ReplacementTerm.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryPreferredTerm

attributeDefs.description: The description field can be used to explain why the linked term is of interest.
attributeDefs.name: description

attributeDefs.description: The steward is the person responsible for assessing the relationship and deciding if it should be approved or not
attributeDefs.name: steward

relationshipDefs.description: RelatedTerm is a relationship used to say that the linked glossary term may also be of interest. It is like a 'see also' link in a dictionary.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryRelatedTerm

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the data field and the dataset
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_field_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: TermCategorization is a relationship used to organize terms into categories. A term may be linked with many categories and a category may have many terms linked to it. This relationship may connect terms and categories both in the same glossary or in different glossaries.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryTermCategorization

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the collection and the dataset
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_dataset_assignment

attributeDefs.description: The description field can be used to add details about the relationship.
attributeDefs.name: description

attributeDefs.description: The steward is the person responsible for assessing the relationship and deciding if it should be approved or not.
attributeDefs.name: steward

relationshipDefs.description: Synonym is a relationship between glossary terms that have the same, or a very similar meaning in the same language. Notice that both ends of this relationship have the same name and refer to the same type; this results in one Synonym attribute being added to GlossaryTerm.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossarySynonym

attributeDefs.description: The description field can be used to add details about the relationship.
attributeDefs.name: description

attributeDefs.description: The steward is the person responsible for assessing the relationship and deciding if it should be approved or not
attributeDefs.name: steward

relationshipDefs.description: ReplacementTerm is a relationship that indicates that the replacementTerm must be used instead of the replacedByTerm. This is stronger version of the PreferredTerm.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryReplacementTerm

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the data entity and the data attribute
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_data_entity_attribute_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the data attribute and its business data steward
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_data_attribute_steward_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the data attribute and the data field
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_data_attribute_field_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the data field to other data entities
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_field_parent_assignment

attributeDefs.description: The description field can be used to add details about the relationship.
attributeDefs.name: description

attributeDefs.description: The steward is the person responsible for assessing the relationship and deciding if it should be approved or not
attributeDefs.name: steward

relationshipDefs.description: Translation is a relationship that defines that the related terms represent the same meaning, but each are written in a different language. Hence one is a translation of the other. The language of each term is defined in the Glossary object that anchors the term.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryTranslation

attributeDefs.description: The description field can be used to add details about the relationship.
attributeDefs.name: description

attributeDefs.description: The steward is the person responsible for assessing the relationship and deciding if it should be approved or not.
attributeDefs.name: steward

relationshipDefs.description: ValidValue is a relationship that shows the validValue term represents one of the valid values that could be assigned to a data item that has the meaning described in the validValueFor term.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryValidValue

attributeDefs.description: The description field can be used to add details about the semantic assignment.
attributeDefs.name: description

attributeDefs.description: The confidence attribute in the semantic assignment stores the level of confidence (0-100%) in the correctness of the semantic assignment - it is typically used by discovery engines.
attributeDefs.name: confidence

attributeDefs.description: The semantic assignment is created by the user (person or engine) identified by the createdBy attribute.
attributeDefs.name: createdBy

attributeDefs.description: The steward is the person responsible for assessing the semantic assignment and deciding if it should be approved or not.
attributeDefs.name: steward

relationshipDefs.description: SemanticAssignment is a relationship used to assign a term to a referenceable object. This means that the term describes the meaning of the referenceable object. The semantic assignment needs to be a controlled relationship when glossary definitions are used to provide classifications for the data assets and hence define how the data is to be governed.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossarySemanticAssignment

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the data field and the data quality rule
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_data_quality_field_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the dataset to other datasets
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_dataset_parent_assignment

relationshipDefs.description: The relationship between the system and the collection
relationshipDefs.name: m4i_collection_assignment

attributeDefs.description: The description field can be used to add details about the relationship.
attributeDefs.name: description

attributeDefs.description: The steward is the person responsible for assessing the relationship and deciding if it should be approved or not.
attributeDefs.name: steward

relationshipDefs.description: IsA is a relationship that defines that the 'isA' term is a more generic term than the 'isOf' term. For example, this relationship would be use to say that 'Cat' ISA 'Animal'.
relationshipDefs.name: AtlasGlossaryIsARelationship

structDefs.description: Atlas Type representing a tag/value pair associated with an AWS object, eg S3 bucket
structDefs.name: aws_tag

structDefs.name: aws_cloud_watch_metric

structDefs.name: aws_s3_access_policy

structDefs.name: aws_s3_bucket_lifeCycleRule
